channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel cuts off Gaza aid to pressure Hamas to accept new ceasefire proposal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel stopped the entry of all goods and supplies into the Gaza Strip on Sunday and warned of “additional consequences” if Hamas doesn't accept a new proposal to extend a fragile ceasefire.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Uruguay's new leftist president takes office, facing a financial balancing act
item.description: MONTEVIDEO, Uruguay (AP) — Yamandú Orsi, a telegenic left-leaning former mayor and history teacher, took office as Uruguay's new president on Saturday, at the helm of a government that has pledged to strengthen the social safety net while reversing years of economic stagnation.
item.dc:creator: MATILDE CAMPODONICO and ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel agrees to Trump envoy’s temporary Gaza ceasefire extension proposal as first phase expires: reports
item.description: As the first phase of the temporary ceasefire in Gaza ends, the Israeli government said Saturday night it had agreed to a proposal by Trump envoy Steve Witkoff to extend it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UK, Ukraine sign loan agreement after 'meaningful and warm' visit
item.description: The United Kingdom agreed to loan Ukraine around $3 billion on Saturday to support their efforts in the war against Russia after a contentious White House meeting left the world wondering about the nation’s ability to secure its sovereign borders.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel supports a US proposal to extend Gaza ceasefire's first phase. But Hamas wants Phase 2
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Israel’s government said early Sunday it supports a proposal to extend the first phase of the ceasefire in Gaza through Ramadan and Passover, though Hamas has insisted on negotiating the truce’s second phase instead.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, The Hill

item.title: Turkey's Kurds wary of path to peace after PKK declares ceasefire
item.description: DIYARBAKIR, Turkey (AP) — A momentous ceasefire declaration on Saturday stirred a mix of emotions in southeast Turkey and northern Iraq, where people bore the brunt of the 40-year conflict between Kurdish militants and the Turkish state.
item.dc:creator: MUCAHIT CEYLAN and ADNAN AHMAD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syrians begin fasting during first Ramadan without Assad family rule in decades
item.description: Some restaurants and coffee shops in Syria were closed during the day as observant Muslims began fasting during the holy month of Ramadan.
item.dc:creator: Abdulrahman Shaheen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ukrainians rally around Zelenskyy as defender of national interest after Oval Office blowout
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Soon after Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy left the White House on Friday after an astonishing Oval Office blowout with President Donald Trump, Ukrainians rallied around Zelenskyy as a defender of his country's interests.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World leaders back Zelenskyy following Trump, Vance Oval Office spat
item.description: World leaders pledge support for Ukraine and emphasize Russia is the "aggressor" following the Oval Office spat in which the Trump administration accused President Volodymyr Zelenskyy of being "disrespectful.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bodies of 12 Pakistani migrants who died in boat sinking have been repatriated
item.description: PARACHINAR, Pakistan (AP) — The bodies of 12 out of at least 16 Pakistanis who died this month when a boat carrying dozens of Europe-bound migrants sank off Libya's coast have been repatriated to Pakistan, officials said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis' condition improving but will remain 'guarded' until he is stable for several days: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis is improving after spending the past two weeks hospitalized with double pneumonia, but the Vatican said his prognosis is "guarded" until he's stable for a few more days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Morocco urges people to not buy sheep for Eid al-Adha celebrations
item.description: OULAD SLAMA, Morocco (AP) — Sheep come running when Larbi El Ghazouani pours alfalfa and straw into their troughs twice a day.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ and AKRAM OUBACHIR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin 'doesn't want peace,' EU chief diplomat warns ahead of Trump, Zelenskyy deal
item.description: The EU's chief diplomat has warned President Trump that Russian President Putin "doesn't really want peace" as Trump heads into a deal with Ukrainian President Zelenskyy.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Panama and Costa Rica turning into a 'black hole' for migrants and deportees from US, observers warn
item.description: MIRAMAR, Panama (AP) — Officials in Costa Rica and Panama are confiscating migrants’ passports and cellphones, denying them access to legal services and moving them between remote outposts as they wrestle with the logistics of a suddenly reversed migration flow.
item.dc:creator: MEGAN JANETSKY, MATÍAS DELACROIX and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tens of thousands of Palestinians seek shelter after Israeli assaults across the West Bank
item.description: JENIN, West Bank (AP) — When Israeli snipers took positions in her neighborhood, Haleemeh Zawaydeh knew her family needed to leave quickly. As the snipers' gunfire rang out, the 63-year-old matriarch said there was no time to pack as she and 14 other family members fled on foot.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, AREF TUFAHA and JALAL BWAITEL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexico extradites dozens of cartel leaders and members to US, including drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero
item.description: As Mexico and the U.S. negotiate trade tariffs, the Mexican government has handed over 29 cartel leaders and members who were wanted in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US wants to end Ukraine war with little involvement: Ex-ambassador
item.description: President Donald Trump has met with European leaders during the week to discuss ending the war quickly.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: The Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, arrive in Florida
item.description: After the brothers arrived in Florida, state Attorney General James Uthmeier said in a social media post that his office would conduct a “preliminary inquiry” into them.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis continues to improve but his prognosis remains guarded: Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis continued his slow recovery from double pneumonia on Thursday, beating back speculation of an imminent death, resignation or conclave and signalling that he was still very much in charge albeit in a weakened state.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy expected to sign minerals deal Friday: Trump
item.description: Details are vague but what is known is that the United States is requesting revenue from Ukraine's rare minerals.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: US and Ukraine near an economic deal with mineral rights but no security promise, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine and the U.S. have reached an agreement on a framework for a broad economic deal that would include access to Ukraine's rare earth minerals, three senior Ukrainian officials said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SUSIE BLANN, HANNA ARHIROVA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Man forced to sit next to corpse after woman died midway through flight
item.description: Passenger Mitchell Ring said the flight crew did everything they could to revive the woman but they were unsuccessful.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Former Denver developer shot dead at luxury Belize home in likely 'hit': Police
item.description: Boris Mannsfeld was found shot to death at The Villas at Cocoplum, a luxury residential community on the Caribbean country's southeastern coast, Belizean media outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Factor, NewsNation

item.title: Animal rights groups, prosecutors trying to stop sale of exotic animals in Tijuana
item.description: "Recently there was a video on social media of someone walking through a mall with a white tiger on a leash," said Ortiz González.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation
